title: cerastium glomeratum f. apetalum
intro: Cerastium glomeratum f. apetalum, also known as Sticky Mouse-ear or Common Mouse-ear, is a flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) and is characterized by its sticky hairs and small white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Cerastium glomeratum f. apetalum are said to provide health benefits including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used in folk remedies to treat a range of ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
